mainEntity.name: What are my workers' compensation rights after a forklift accident at work in Murray UT?
mainEntity.text: What are my workers' compensation rights after a forklift accident at work in Murray UT?

acceptedAnswer.text: In Utah, forklift accidents on the job qualify for workers' comp covering medical bills, lost wages (66.67% of average weekly wage), and disability benefits if reported within 180 days and filed within 3 years. No-fault system; employer cannot retaliate.

suggestedAnswer.text: Utah's workers' compensation is no-fault, meaning you receive benefits regardless of fault for forklift injuries. Benefits include full medical treatment, temporary total disability (2/3 wages up to state max ~$1,000/week in 2026), permanent partial if applicable. Report injury to employer immediately; file claim with Utah Labor Commission within 3 years but ideally within 180 days for full coverage. Consult attorney to maximize claim against insurance delays common in warehouse-heavy areas like Murray.
